subject: Project Management
title: Gemba: Go and See
description: A micro-lesson that makes Gemba: Go and See usable.

hook.text: Gemba means 'the real place.' In lean thinking, managers must go where the work actually happens.

buildup.text: Ohno made managers stand in a chalk circle on the factory floor and observe for hours. Just watch.

discovery.text: Problems visible on the floor are invisible in spreadsheets. The truth lives where work is done.

twist.text: Most management decisions are made in meeting rooms — far from the gemba. That's why they often fail.

climax.text: Going to gemba isn't a technique — it's a philosophy: respect the people doing the work and learn from them.

punchline.text: The boardroom guesses. The factory floor knows.

quiz.question: What is 'gemba' in lean thinking?
options: The actual place where work happens — managers must go observe
A digital dashboard for tracking metrics
